title: agrostis scabra var. keweenawensis
intro: Agrostis scabra var. keweenawensis is a rare, endangered variety of the bentgrass plant, native to the Upper Peninsula of Michigan in the United States. It is a perennial grass that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating digestive issues and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is rich in bioactive compounds that offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and soothing skin irritations. Agrostis scabra var. keweenawensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for the extraction of its medicinal properties and utilization for therapeutic purposes.
